description:
You are a Flight Dynamics Engineer at a space science institute. The new EXHEA space telescope will be placed in a Highly Elliptical Orbit (HEO). Third-body perturbations from the Sun and Moon (Kozai-Lidov cycles) will cause the eccentricity to oscillate. If not configured correctly, the perigee will drop into the Earth's atmosphere, destroying the spacecraft before its 5-year mission ends. Read the mission specification at ~/Desktop/exhea_mission_spec.txt. Using GMAT, model the mission using only point mass gravity for Earth, Sun, and Moon (excluding drag and SRP). Determine the baseline lifetime in days for an initial RAAN of 0.0 degrees, defined as the time until the altitude drops below 120 km. Find a stable RAAN that ensures the spacecraft survives the full 1825-day mission without reentering. Save the successful GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/stable_heo_mission.script and write an analysis report to ~/GMAT_output/heo_stability_report.txt with exactly these lines:
Baseline_Lifetime_Days: <value>
Stable_RAAN_deg: <value>

Login: username=ga, password=password123
